title: Responses, Problem Details, Streaming, and Protocol-Aware Output · Language Lens: Error Taxonomy Mapping
summary: A service writes bodies before status and headers, exposes stack traces, treats cancellation as 500, flushes unbounded streams, and assumes optional ResponseWriter interfaces always exist. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo error taxonomy mapping increment in single-commit response and streaming contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Map validation, authentication, authorization, absence, conflict, precondition, limit, timeout, cancellation, dependency, and internal failures consistently.
Serialize RFC 9457 problem type, title, status, detail, instance, extensions, and application/problem+json without leaking implementation data.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Error Taxonomy Mapping · Theory Error Taxonomy Mapping
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6665 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to choose one response shape and bound streaming flush evidence; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
